missed_changes: Launched a prominent anti-degen / anti-casino crusade in Dec 2023 — explicitly attacking memecoins, 100x leverage, yield farming, airdrop farming, and 'casino' speculation as the 'primary force holding the space back' (new, sharply-stated quality/purpose stance not anticipated)
Made modular vs. monolithic architecture and the 'modular tech stack' a central recurring theme (Modularity Paradox, UNIX philosophy, 'interface that de-constrains', a16z big ideas authored entry)

Became actively enthusiastic about a specific cross-chain bridge (LayerZero v1/v2, Google Cloud integration) — notable given his prior generalized distrust of bridges; framed approval around improved trust assumptions
Emphasized 'underexplored' mechanisms — token lockups, retroactive funding, non-transferable tokens, on-chain reputation, rewards for verifiable work — as the constructive alternative to speculation
notes: Overwhelmingly confirmatory window. Ali stayed on his sector/technology theses (AI×crypto via Gensyn-style markets, ZK/zkML, privacy via Aztec, L2/multi-chain scaling, dev platforms/Crypto Startup School, music/NFTs via doubled-down Sound.xyz Series A) and on his network-level token convictions (ETH, SOL via the Toly debate, NEAR's modular-Ethereum alignment). Risk-on builder posture confirmed via continued active deployment (Sound, Freatic, Alongside) and 'only the beginning' framing. The bridge-security concern reads confirmed at stance level — he keeps applying the 'who do you have to trust' lens, praising LZ v2 for removing relayer trust. Wallet-signing safety and DePIN appear only in others' authored sections or bare category lists, so marked absent for Ali personally. The biggest unanticipated development is his vocal anti-speculation campaign, which is consistent with (not contradictory to) his builder-horizon risk-on framing.
